name: Birkin 35 leather handbag
description:
This is an authentic HERMES Swift Birkin 35 in Pelouse. Hermès Swift leather (formerly Gulliver) is a smooth, fine-grained calfskin reintroduced in 2005, prized for its buttery-soft texture, slouchy structure, and exceptional ability to absorb vibrant colors.  This sophisticated handbag is expertly crafted from green smooth, supple leather. The bag features rolled leather top handles, a frontal flap, a hanging clochette, and polished palladium hardware, including a turn lock, padlock, and key. The top flap opens to a matching leather interior with a zipper and patch pockets. Date code square M. 
Hermès Pelouse emerges as one of the most evocatively verdant and expressively pastoral hues in the luxury house’s distinguished color portfolio. This sophisticated, lawn-inspired shade encapsulates a compelling landscape narrative—simultaneously referencing the manicured perfection of cultivated grounds and the refined natural splendor of European garden traditions—creating a color that projects curated botanical elegance while maintaining the sublime execution synonymous with the heritage brand. Pelouse occupies a distinctive position in the Hermès spectrum—a refined, landscape-derived hue with remarkable chromatic vitality that draws inspiration from both aristocratic garden heritage and contemporary appreciation of manicured natural aesthetics while embodying the meticulous sophistication expected from this storied maison.
What distinguishes Pelouse is its exceptional landscape resonance and cultivated authenticity. Unlike more conventionally green Hermès colors such as the raw Vert Cru or the deep Vert Foncé, Pelouse offers a more distinctly curated quality with substantial chromatic vibrancy that gives it a sophisticated, almost horticulturally precise character. This perfectly calibrated color profile allows Pelouse to function as both a heritage landscape statement and a versatile expression of refined natural cultivation—an evocative choice for the Hermès client who values colors with horticultural significance and authentic garden-inspired refinement.
